client_context.ai_history: Scenario depicts a global CPG manufacturer adopting GenAI for packaging automation. Interview context: Adobe enterprise role leveraging AWS + GenAI.

problem_statement.title: Product Packaging Variant Generation at Scale via GenAI
problem_statement.summary: A global CPG manufacturer needs to generate hundreds of localized product packaging variants monthly across regional markets. Current manual design processes are slow, expensive, error-prone, and create bottlenecks in approval cycles. The PoC demonstrates an automated GenAI pipeline that accepts SKU briefs, generates region-specific packaging designs in multiple aspect ratios, and organizes outputs in S3.

current_state.process_description: Design teams manually create and localize packaging variants for hundreds of SKUs per month using decentralized design processes and multiple agencies. Regulatory review and brand approval involve multiple stakeholders per region, creating bottlenecks.
pain_points: Manual packaging design overload: creating/localizing variants for hundreds of SKUs is slow, expensive, and error-prone
Inconsistent quality and compliance: off-brand or non-compliant packaging due to decentralized processes
Slow approval cycles: bottlenecks with multiple stakeholders in each region
Difficulty tracking regulatory compliance at scale: siloed files and manual compliance checking
Resource drain: skilled design teams overloaded with repetitive layout/resizing instead of strategic creative work
current_state.volume: Hundreds of localized variants per month across regional markets

desired_state.description: Automated GenAI pipeline that generates packaging variants from SKU briefs, maintains brand consistency, produces multiple aspect ratios, and organizes outputs systematically in S3. Frees design teams for strategic creative work.
target_improvements: Accelerate time-to-market for regional packaging variants
Ensure brand consistency across all markets via automated generation
Maximize local relevance through region-specific imagery and messaging
Reduce manual rework and approval cycle time
Track design effectiveness and compliance at scale

ai_suitability_assessment.rationale: GenAI image generation is the core technology for this use case. The task involves generating visual variants from structured inputs (SKU briefs), which is a direct application of generative AI. All 6 suitability criteria score YES: repeatable generation task, structured input data available, current human process is inconsistent, clear time/cost savings, error tolerances compatible with iterative generation, and human review gates prevent autonomous risk.
favorable_factors: Image generation from structured prompts is a proven GenAI capability
Repeatable task: same pipeline processes different SKU briefs
Structured input (JSON SKU briefs) enables consistent prompt construction
Multiple aspect ratio generation is a natural fit for image generation APIs
Human review gates for brand compliance reduce autonomous decision risk
Measurable ROI: variants per hour vs. manual design time
risk_factors: GenAI image quality may not meet premium packaging standards without fine-tuning
Brand consistency requires careful prompt engineering or reference image conditioning
Regulatory text overlay (allergens, ingredients) needs precise placement — Pillow handles this reliably vs. relying on GenAI for text
PoC scope limits validation of production-scale reliability

pain_points: Manual packaging design overload: creating and localizing design variants for hundreds of SKUs per month is slow, expensive, and error-prone (Classification: Process inefficiency; Impact: days per variant; Pattern: GenAI automated generation)

Inconsistent quality and compliance: risk of off-brand, non-compliant packaging due to decentralized design processes and multiple agencies (Classification: Data quality / Compliance risk; Impact: brand damage, regulatory delays; Pattern: Automated brand compliance checks)

Slow approval cycles: bottlenecks in regulatory review and brand approval with multiple stakeholders per region (Classification: Process inefficiency; Impact: weeks of delay per cycle; Pattern: Automated pre-screening + approval tracking)

Difficulty tracking regulatory compliance at scale: siloed design files and manual compliance checking (Classification: Compliance risk / Integration gap; Impact: non-compliant designs reaching production; Pattern: Centralized asset management + compliance logging)

Resource drain: skilled design and brand teams overloaded with repetitive layout/resizing requests instead of strategic creative work (Classification: Process inefficiency / Knowledge loss; Impact: reduced creative output; Pattern: GenAI handles repetitive variants, humans focus on creative direction)

success_criteria.description: PoC accepts SKU brief JSON and produces packaging images in 3 aspect ratios
success_criteria.measurement: Binary: pipeline runs end-to-end for at least 2 products

success_criteria.description: Pipeline processes at least 2 different products/flavors from a single SKU brief
success_criteria.measurement: Count of distinct products with generated packaging

success_criteria.description: Generated assets are saved to S3 organized by SKU, Region, and format
success_criteria.measurement: Binary: S3 structure matches SKU/Region/format hierarchy

success_criteria.description: README covers how to run, example I/O, key decisions, and assumptions
success_criteria.measurement: Count of required README sections present

success_criteria.description: AWS resources deployed via CloudFormation (not Terraform)
success_criteria.measurement: Binary: CloudFormation stack deploys to CREATE_COMPLETE

success_criteria.description: Each generated packaging image is visually inspectable and displays product name, at least one key attribute, and regulatory text in legible form.
success_criteria.measurement: Human visual inspection of generated images — pass if product name and attributes are readable

functional_requirements.title: SKU Brief JSON Input
functional_requirements.description: Accept a structured JSON SKU brief containing: product(s) with at least 2 different products/flavors, target region/market, target audience/demographics, and key product attributes (e.g., organic, vegan, seasonal).
acceptance_criteria: Accepts valid JSON SKU brief
Supports at least 2 products/flavors per brief
Parses region, audience, and attribute fields

functional_requirements.title: S3 Asset Input and Reuse
functional_requirements.description: Accept input brand assets stored in AWS S3 and reuse them when available for packaging generation.
acceptance_criteria: Reads brand assets from a configured S3 bucket
Detects when existing assets are available and reuses them
Falls through to generation when assets are missing

functional_requirements.title: GenAI Image Generation
functional_requirements.description: When brand assets are missing, generate new packaging assets using Amazon Nova Canvas (primary) or Titan Image Generator V2 (fallback) via AWS Bedrock in us-east-1. Retry/backoff on throttling. Cache results locally.
acceptance_criteria: Calls Amazon Nova Canvas (amazon.nova-canvas-v1:0) as primary model in us-east-1
Falls back to Titan V2 (amazon.titan-image-generator-v2:0) on failure
Exponential backoff: 3 attempts, 2^n seconds between retries
Results cached to ~/.cache/pai-pipeline/ by content hash

functional_requirements.title: Multi-Aspect-Ratio Packaging Output
functional_requirements.description: Create packaging designs in at least 3 aspect ratios: front label (1:1, 1024x1024), back label (9:16, 576x1024), and wraparound (16:9, 1024x576).
acceptance_criteria: Generates at least 3 distinct aspect ratios per product
Each output is a valid PNG image file

functional_requirements.title: Product Messaging and Regulatory Info Overlay
functional_requirements.description: Display product messaging, key attributes, and regulatory information on the final packaging designs using Pillow text overlay. English at minimum.
acceptance_criteria: Product name, key attributes visible on packaging
Regulatory information (allergens, certifications) displayed
Text is legible and appropriately placed for each aspect ratio
English language at minimum

functional_requirements.title: AWS Execution Environment
functional_requirements.description: Pipeline runs in AWS (us-east-1). Invoked via Claude Code custom skills. No argparse or CLI framework. AWS resources provisioned via CloudFormation. Budget unconstrained.
acceptance_criteria: Invoked via Claude Code /run-pipeline skill
AWS resources deployed via CloudFormation to us-east-1
Uses AWS CLI named profile 'pai-exercise' for credential management
Credentials sourced from ~/.aws/credentials — never hardcoded

functional_requirements.title: Organized S3 Output Storage
functional_requirements.description: Save generated packaging outputs to an AWS S3 folder, clearly organized by SKU, Region, and format.
acceptance_criteria: Outputs saved to S3 bucket
Folder hierarchy: {SKU}/{Region}/{format}/
File naming convention is clear and consistent

functional_requirements.title: README Documentation
functional_requirements.description: Include comprehensive README explaining: how to run, example input/output, key design decisions, and assumptions/limitations.
acceptance_criteria: How to run: setup and execution instructions (5-minute setup flow)
Example input: sample SKU brief JSON
Example output: sample generated packaging images or descriptions
Key design decisions documented (8-10 decisions with rationale)
Assumptions and limitations listed honestly

functional_requirements.title: CI/CD Pipeline
functional_requirements.description: GitHub Actions CI/CD pipeline with automated testing (pytest), linting (ruff/flake8), dependency vulnerability scanning (pip-audit), and CloudFormation deployment workflow.
acceptance_criteria: GitHub Actions ci.yml runs lint and tests on every push
GitHub Actions deploy.yml runs CloudFormation deploy on main push
pip-audit runs in CI and fails on high/critical vulnerabilities
All action versions pinned to SHA hashes

functional_requirements.title: Claude Code Interface Layer
functional_requirements.description: Claude Code CLI with 8 custom skills replaces all CLI/argparse interaction. Skills are defined in .claude/skills/ in the target repo. No argparse or CLI framework is used.
acceptance_criteria: 8 custom skills defined: /run-pipeline, /pipeline-status, /view-results, /deploy, /teardown, /health-check, /run-tests, /generate-demo
Skills invokable from Claude Code CLI in target repo
/run-pipeline accepts sku-brief-path argument
/deploy and /teardown have disable-model-invocation: true

functional_requirements.title: Automated QA Hooks
functional_requirements.description: Claude Code hooks in .claude/settings.json enforce quality automatically: auto-lint on file edit/write, test gate on stop, destructive command guard on bash.
acceptance_criteria: PostToolUse on Edit|Write triggers auto-lint on changed files
Stop hook runs test suite and blocks completion if tests fail
PreToolUse on Bash validates no destructive AWS commands without confirmation

functional_requirements.title: AWS MCP Server Integration
functional_requirements.description: AWS MCP servers configured in .mcp.json in the target repo. Enables natural language AWS administration via Claude Code. Uses uv-based Python packages (not npm).
acceptance_criteria: awslabs.aws-iac-mcp-server@latest configured for CloudFormation and IaC assistance
AWS Knowledge MCP Server configured (remote HTTP via fastmcp proxy)
All servers use Windows stdio transport with uv tool run command
No deprecated servers (Cloud Control API, CDK, Terraform) included

functional_requirements.title: Brand Compliance Checks
functional_requirements.description: Optional (Phase 6): check generated packaging for brand compliance — logo presence, required color usage, font consistency. Uses Claude via Bedrock as LLM-as-judge.
acceptance_criteria: Detects presence/absence of brand logo
Validates color palette against brand guidelines
Reports compliance status per generated asset

functional_requirements.title: Regulatory Content Checks
functional_requirements.description: Optional (Phase 6): flag missing allergen disclosures, ingredient lists, or certifications for target regions using a synthetic JSON lookup table.
acceptance_criteria: Checks for required regulatory content per region
Flags missing allergen or ingredient information
Reports regulatory compliance status

functional_requirements.title: Approval Status Tracking
functional_requirements.description: Optional (Phase 6): track and log approval status for generated designs and generation results via manifest.json append — no separate database.
acceptance_criteria: Logs generation results (success/failure, parameters used)
Tracks approval status per generated asset via manifest.json
Status queryable via /pipeline-status skill

security.authorization_model: IAM policies — least privilege for S3 and Bedrock access. Named profile 'pai-exercise'.
encryption_requirements: S3 Block Public Access enabled on all buckets

model_quality.primary_model_rationale: Amazon Nova Canvas scores TIFA 0.897 and ImageReward 1.250 per Amazon Nova Technical Report (arxiv 2506.12103). Higher ImageReward than competing tier-equivalent models. Built-in inpainting/outpainting for future enhancement. Only available in us-east-1 — confirms region choice.
model_quality.fallback_rationale: Available in us-east-1. Sufficient for dev/iterate tiers. Lower cost per image.
model_quality.removed_rationale: Only available in us-west-2 (confirmed via AWS docs). No cross-region inference profile available. Nova Canvas scores higher on available benchmarks. SD3.5 Large moved to BACKLOG for future consideration if cross-region inference becomes available.

sources.name: Brand Assets
sources.quality_notes: Client-provided brand images (logos, product photos). Synthetic/placeholder for PoC.

sources.name: GenAI Image API (Bedrock)
sources.volume: 6-12 images per pipeline run (3 aspect ratios x 2-4 products)
sources.quality_notes: Primary: Nova Canvas. Fallback: Titan V2. Both in us-east-1.

sources.name: Pipeline Run Manifest
sources.quality_notes: Written to S3 and local outputs/runs/. Enables run history without a database.

integration_points.protocol: AWS SDK (boto3 for image gen; anthropic[bedrock] for text reasoning)

integration_points.auth_method: GitHub Secrets (AWS credentials) or OIDC federation

constraints.budget_range: Unconstrained — optimize for quality and development speed. Expected: $5-25 for PoC run (Bedrock API costs). No Free Tier restriction.
constraints.timeline_note: Interview take-home exercise — AI-accelerated delivery, estimated 1 work day with Claude Code assistance. 0 = deadline not formally specified; submission timing is interview-driven.
technology_restrictions: CloudFormation for IaC (not Terraform — simpler for PoC, self-contained YAML)
GitHub for source code hosting (public repo: praeducer/pai-take-home-exercise)
Claude Code CLI with custom skills as interface layer — no argparse or Click CLI framework
AWS us-east-1 as primary region (Nova Canvas + Titan V2 + Claude all available)
constraints.team_constraints: Solo developer with AI-assisted development (Claude Code + Sonnet). AI handles all coding, testing, and scaffolding. Human handles: AWS console setup (Bedrock model access), credential configuration (aws configure), GitHub repo creation, approval gates.

in_scope: GenAI packaging image generation pipeline (Nova Canvas primary, Titan V2 fallback)
SKU brief JSON input parsing and schema validation
S3 input asset retrieval and reuse
Product messaging and regulatory text overlay via Pillow
Claude Code custom skills as interface layer (8 skills)
AWS MCP servers for natural language AWS administration (.mcp.json)
Pipeline run manifest (flat JSON, no database)
README documentation with examples and design decisions
out_of_scope: argparse or Click CLI framework (replaced by Claude Code skills)
PostgreSQL or any relational database (flat JSON + S3 only for PoC)
scope_boundaries.justification: Scoped to a PoC demonstrating technical approach, GenAI integration capability, and AI-native development practices. Production features (scale, multi-user, analytics, relational DB) and out-of-region models are outside PoC scope. Budget unconstrained — quality and speed are the optimization targets.

assumptions: AWS account 730007904340 is available with Bedrock model access enableable in us-east-1
Amazon Nova Canvas (amazon.nova-canvas-v1:0) and Titan V2 (amazon.titan-image-generator-v2:0) must be explicitly enabled in the AWS Bedrock console before pipeline use
Claude Sonnet 4.6 (anthropic.claude-sonnet-4-6) must be explicitly enabled in the AWS Bedrock console
SKU brief JSON schema is defined by the developer (not provided by the interviewer)
Brand assets for the PoC can be synthetic/placeholder images
Regulatory information overlay is text-based (not from a real regulatory database)
The PoC demonstrates the pipeline pattern — production quality images are not expected
Single-region deployment (us-east-1) is sufficient for the PoC
Interview panel evaluates technical approach, code quality, and architecture decisions — not pixel-perfect design output
AI-assisted development hours (Claude Code + Sonnet) are the operative unit for estimates — not raw human labor hours
GitHub repo praeducer/pai-take-home-exercise is public — no secrets or credentials committed
uv (Python package manager for MCP server execution) and AWS CLI 2.x+ are available. Node.js is not required — AWS MCP servers use uv tool run.

_metadata.author: sa-agent
_metadata.changelog: v2.0: CI/CD upgraded to must_have (FR-009), Claude Code interface added (FR-010), QA hooks added (FR-011), AWS MCP servers added (FR-012), old FR-009-011 renumbered FR-013-015. SC-006 added. NFR-008 model quality added. Budget unconstrained. Nova Canvas primary model (SD3.5 removed — us-west-2 only). Region confirmed us-east-1.
